explanation: The property states that if the power button is pushed (changes from false to true), then eventually the system will enter Start-up Mode. It also ensures that if the power button is already true, the system will eventually enter Start-up Mode. This accurately captures that the pushing of the power button allows the transition to Start-up Mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the power button is pressed (bool_exp1), then in the next state (X), the system will be in Start-up Mode (bool_exp3). This directly translates 'The transition to Start-up Mode shall be allowed by pushing the power button' by modeling 'pushing the power button' as the cause that immediately leads to the system being in 'Start-up Mode'.

The property states that globally, if the system is currently 'off' and the 'powerButton' is pressed, then in the next state, the system will transition to 'StartUpMode'. This directly captures the requirement that pushing the power button when the system is off allows the transition to Start-up Mode.

The natural language statement implies that pushing the power button directly enables or leads to the system entering Start-up Mode. The chosen LTL template `G (bool_exp1 -> (X bool_exp3))` perfectly captures this causal relationship. It means "Globally, if the power button is pressed (bool_exp1), then in the very next state, the system will be in Start-up Mode (bool_exp3)". This models an immediate and consistent transition upon the activation of the power button.

The property states that globally, if the system is currently off and the power button is pushed, then in the very next state, the system must transition to Start-up Mode. This captures the requirement that pushing the power button from an off state allows and triggers the transition to Start-up Mode.

The property states that if the power button is pushed (i.e., it transitions from not being pressed to being pressed, captured by `(!powerButton & X powerButton)`), then in the next state, it must eventually lead to the system entering Start-up Mode (`X (F StartUpMode)`). The second part `(powerButton -> (F StartUpMode))` ensures that even if the power button is already pressed in the initial state, the system will eventually enter Start-up Mode. This accurately captures the requirement that pushing the power button allows the transition to Start-up Mode.

The property states that globally, if the power button is pushed ('powerButton' is true), then in the very next state, the system will be in Start-up Mode ('StartUpMode' is true). This accurately captures the immediate causal relationship described: pushing the button allows, or directly leads to, the transition into Start-up Mode.

The property states that globally, whenever the power button is pressed, the system will enter Start-up Mode in the very next state. This accurately captures the meaning that pushing the power button allows or triggers the transition to Start-up Mode.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the power button is pressed (bool_exp2), then eventually (F) the system will enter Start-up Mode (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that pushing the power button should lead to the transition into Start-up Mode, indicating it is an allowed and expected consequence.

The property states that globally, if the power button is pressed (powerButton), then in the next state (X), the system will be in Start-up Mode (StartUpMode). This captures the requirement that pushing the power button allows or initiates the transition to Start-up Mode.
